summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Chi-square goodness of fit test'?

"Chi-square goodness of fit test" means: Tests H₀ that a categorical variable follows a specified distribution by comparing observed and expected counts. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Chi-square statistic'?

"Chi-square statistic" means: χ² = Σ(O - E)²/E; sums the squared standardized differences between observed (O) and expected (E) counts across all categories. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Expected counts'?

"Expected counts" means: E = n × p_i for each category; the counts predicted by the null hypothesis distribution. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Conditions'?

"Conditions" means: Random sample, all expected counts ≥ 5 (some textbooks use ≥ 1 with no more than 20% below 5), and independent observations. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Degrees of freedom'?

In Chi-Square Goodness of Fit, "Degrees of freedom" refers to: df = k - 1, where k is the number of categories; determines the shape of the chi-square distribution.
